[Wakili Notes — WhatsApp export, group "Finance Bill — Families"]

24/06/2024, 8:15 PM - Amina (mother of Brian): They have not been released
and the station says come tomorrow. The OCS at Central will not give us any
OB number. Brian was arrested at Parliament Road around 2 PM.

24/06/2024, 8:42 PM - Mrs. Akinyi (mother of Cynthia): Same here. They told
us to wait. No advocate has been allowed in. The officer at the desk —
Cpl. Otieno — told us to leave.

24/06/2024, 9:01 PM - Hassan (father of Musa): Advocate Wanjiku is at the
station now. She says they refused her access on instructions from the OCS.

25/06/2024, 9:02 AM - Brian's auntie: Advocate Wanjiku says we need the OB
number and the names of officers. Please share whatever you have.

25/06/2024, 9:14 AM - Amina: I have a photo of the bruising on Brian's
shoulder from yesterday. He was hit by a baton. Will share when I can get
to wifi.

25/06/2024, 11:30 AM - Mrs. Akinyi: Cynthia called from a borrowed phone.
She says they have not been given food since yesterday. They are six of
them held together. She says PC Kariuki and PC Njoroge are the ones who
arrested them. She says they were taken to Central.

26/06/2024, 6:30 PM - Amina: We have photos from Nairobi CBD and the clinic
slip. Brian has been moved to Kamukunji. The advocate was denied again.
Five days now and no magistrate.

26/06/2024, 8:55 PM - Hassan: Musa is at Industrial Area apparently. The
station did not call us. Children are calling each other from any phone
they can borrow.

27/06/2024, 7:00 AM - Mrs. Akinyi: Sgt. Wambui at Pangani at least let me
bring food. Cynthia is okay but Faith was bruised at the protest. Faith
needs medical attention. Sgt. Wambui says station vehicle is unavailable.

28/06/2024, 4:20 PM - Hassan: Advocate Wanjiku is preparing an Article 22
petition. She says we have until around 8 July at the latest. We need to
collect everything: OB extracts (the ones we can get), photos, voice
notes, medical reports.

29/06/2024, 12:40 PM - Amina: Buruburu is letting the advocate see Aisha.
Fifteen minutes only.

30/06/2024, 4:00 PM - Mrs. Akinyi: They have all been moved to Kileleshwa.
The advocate was finally allowed access. Production tomorrow at Milimani.

30/06/2024, 4:18 PM - Amina: Six days. Six days held without charge. We
will file the petition no matter what happens at Milimani.

01/07/2024, 9:30 AM - Hassan: At Milimani. The Chief Magistrate has
released them on bond. The petition is still important — the conduct
during those six days needs a finding.
